meta.title: Cardiology Practice Software | Cardiac RTM & Testing Coordination | clinIQ
meta.description: Complete guide to cardiology practice operations. $10,400/month in cardiac RTM. Testing suite coordination. Reading queue management. Device clinic visibility.

hero.subhead: Your CHF and arrhythmia patients already track symptoms — that's billable cardiac RTM. Your testing suite runs echo, stress, and Holter — that needs coordination. Your device clinic manages hundreds of pacemakers and ICDs — that needs visibility. clinIQ captures RTM revenue and coordinates cardiac operations.

sections.content:
Cardiology practices are operationally complex. Clinic visits, testing (echo, stress, Holter, event monitors), procedures (cath, intervention), and device management all run simultaneously.

**The testing coordination problem:**

Patient needs stress echo. Is the stress lab available? Is the sonographer available? Is the treadmill free? Are results read before the follow-up visit?

Without visibility, testing falls into gaps. Patients wait for scheduling. Results sit unread. Follow-ups happen without results available.

**The RTM opportunity:**

CHF patients should track daily weights and symptoms. Arrhythmia patients should track episodes and triggers. Hypertension patients should track blood pressure. This monitoring qualifies for Remote Therapeutic Monitoring — a revenue stream most cardiology practices don't capture.

**The device clinic challenge:**

Hundreds of patients with pacemakers, ICDs, and loop recorders. Each needs periodic interrogation. Alerts need review. Patients need follow-up. Managing this volume requires systems.

**The reading queue problem:**

Echos, stress tests, Holters, and event monitors generate readings. Who reads them? When? How quickly do results get to patients and referring providers?

Most practices have a backlog. Reads take 3-5 days. Urgent findings get mixed with routine. The queue is managed by memory and sticky notes.

This guide covers systems that address each challenge.

Remote Therapeutic Monitoring for cardiovascular conditions captures between-visit data that cardiology patients should already be tracking.

**What cardiac patients track:**

CHF (heart failure):
- Daily weights (fluid retention indicator)
- Shortness of breath/dyspnea
- Lower extremity edema
- Medication compliance (especially diuretics)
- Activity tolerance

Arrhythmia (AF, VT, SVT):
- Episode frequency and duration
- Symptoms during episodes
- Triggers identified
- Medication compliance (rate control, anticoagulation)

Hypertension:
- Blood pressure logs
- Medication compliance
- Lifestyle factors

**The CPT codes:**

98975: RTM setup — one-time enrollment. ~$20-25.
98977: RTM device supply (cardiovascular). ~$50-60/month.
98980: RTM treatment management — first 20 minutes. ~$50-70/month.
98981: Additional 20-minute increments.

Typical monthly billing: $110-140/patient.

**The math:**

80 cardiac patients × $130/month = $10,400/month = $124,800/year.

**What clinIQ does for cardiac RTM:**

Condition-specific tracking: CHF patients get weight and symptom prompts. AF patients get episode logging. Each condition gets appropriate questions.

Alert thresholds: Weight gain >3 lbs in 3 days (CHF decompensation)? BP consistently >160 systolic? AF episode lasting >24 hours? Alerts trigger intervention.

Provider dashboard: Stable patients vs concerning trends vs urgent attention needed.

Automatic time tracking: Data review documented toward billing threshold.

**The clinical integration:**

RTM data should inform clinical decisions. Patient weight trending up → increase diuretic before hospitalization. AF frequency increasing → consider ablation referral.

clinIQ integrates RTM findings into the visit workflow — data visible when patient arrives for appointment.

Cardiology testing includes:

- Echocardiography (TTE, TEE, stress echo)
- Stress testing (treadmill, pharmacologic)
- Holter monitoring (24-48 hour)
- Event monitoring (2-4 weeks)
- Cardiac CT and MRI (often referred out)

Each test requires scheduling, equipment availability, technician availability, and provider reading.

**The coordination problem:**

Patient needs stress echo. The scheduler checks the stress lab schedule, the sonographer schedule, and cardiologist reading availability. If any piece is missing, the test doesn't happen.

Or: The test happens, but results aren't read before the follow-up. Patient comes in, cardiologist hasn't seen the echo, visit is wasted.

**What clinIQ does for testing coordination:**

Resource visibility: Stress lab, echo suite, Holter hookup — availability visible when scheduling.

Test tracking pipeline:
- Test ordered
- Test scheduled
- Test completed
- Test read (pending/complete)
- Results communicated
- Follow-up scheduled

Aging alerts: 'Echo completed 5 days ago, not yet read.' 'Holter pending read for 7 days.' Problems surface before they compound.

Pre-visit integration: When patient arrives for cardiology visit, status of pending tests visible. 'Stress echo completed 3/15, read by Dr. Smith, results in chart.'

Cardiologists read a high volume of studies:

- Echocardiograms: 15-30 per day for a busy reader
- Stress tests: 5-15 per day
- Holters and event monitors: Variable

Without queue management, reads pile up. The 'pending read' folder grows. Urgent findings get lost in routine. Turnaround time stretches.

**The backlog problem:**

Friday afternoon: 20 echos pending read.
Monday morning: 40 echos pending read.

Which ones are urgent? The critical AS that needs intervention? The EF of 15% that needs hospitalization? Lost in the pile.

**What clinIQ does for reading queue:**

Centralized queue: All pending reads in one view. Echos, stress tests, Holters — visible.

Prioritization: Urgent studies flagged. Inpatient vs outpatient. Stat requests visible.

Aging visibility: Studies pending >24 hours, >48 hours, >72 hours. Oldest studies rise to top.

Reader assignment: Which cardiologist reads which studies? Workload visible. Overflow can be reassigned.

Read completion tracking: Study read → report signed → results communicated. Full cycle tracked.

**The turnaround goal:**

Industry standard: 24-48 hour turnaround for routine outpatient studies. Same-day for urgent/stat.

Many practices run 3-5 days. That's too slow for patients and referring providers.

Queue visibility enables turnaround improvement because you can see the problem.

Cardiology practices with device management (pacemakers, ICDs, loop recorders) handle hundreds to thousands of devices.

Each device needs:
- Periodic interrogation (in-office or remote)
- Alert review and triage
- Battery/lead monitoring
- Patient follow-up for significant findings

**The volume challenge:**

500 devices × quarterly interrogation = 2,000 interrogations/year = 8/day average.

Plus remote monitoring alerts that need daily review.

Plus urgent findings that need immediate action.

Without systems, device management is overwhelming.

**What clinIQ does for device clinic:**

Device inventory: Every patient, every device, implant date, last interrogation, next due.

Interrogation scheduling: Patients due for interrogation visible. Appointment scheduling integrated.

Remote monitoring triage: Alerts categorized by urgency. Routine findings vs concerning findings vs urgent findings.

Follow-up tracking: Significant finding → patient notified → appointment scheduled → issue addressed.

**The alert fatigue problem:**

Remote monitoring generates many alerts. Most are benign. The challenge is finding the important ones.

clinIQ supports alert triage protocols:
- Auto-dismiss routine findings (e.g., normal battery check)
- Flag concerning findings for review
- Escalate urgent findings for immediate action

**Integration with device vendor systems:**

We can integrate with major device vendor remote monitoring platforms (Medtronic CareLink, Abbott Merlin, Boston Scientific Latitude) to pull alerts into a unified view.

Cardiology visits vary significantly:

- New patient consultation: 45-60 minutes
- Established follow-up: 20-30 minutes
- Pre-cath/pre-procedure: 30 minutes
- Post-procedure: 15-20 minutes
- Echo + visit (same-day): 60-90 minutes total
- Device check: 15-30 minutes

The schedule template rarely accounts for this variation. Providers run behind. Patients wait.

**What clinIQ does for patient flow:**

Visit type awareness: Different visit types have different expected durations. Schedule reflects reality.

Testing integration: When visit includes same-day testing (echo before visit), testing status visible. 'Echo complete, patient ready for cardiologist.'

Wait time tracking: Patient waiting 20+ minutes? Alert before they complain.

Provider queue: Who's ready? Who's in testing? Who's waiting on results?

**The same-day testing workflow:**

Common pattern: Patient arrives → Echo → Wait for read → Cardiologist visit.

Without coordination, patient waits an hour between echo and visit because no one knows the echo is done.

With clinIQ: Echo complete → flagged → read prioritized → results available → patient moved to cardiologist queue.

Time from echo completion to cardiologist visit drops from 60 minutes to 20 minutes.

Cardiology implementation covers RTM, testing coordination, reading queue, and device management.

**Week 1: Configuration**

We map your workflows: clinic visits, testing suite, reading workflow, device clinic (if applicable).

RTM templates configured: CHF, arrhythmia, hypertension.

Reading queue configured: Study types, prioritization rules, reader assignments.

**Week 2: Training**

Clinical staff learns patient flow. Schedulers learn testing coordination. Readers learn queue management. Device clinic staff learns device tracking.

**Week 3: Go-Live**

Live operation with support. RTM enrollment beginning. Testing tracking active. Reading queue running.

**Week 4 and Beyond**

RTM revenue building. Test turnaround improving. Reading backlog clearing.

**ROI Timeline:**

Month 1: Testing coordination immediate. Reading queue visibility immediate.

Month 3: RTM billing at scale. Reading turnaround measurably improved. Device clinic organized.

Month 6: Full RTM revenue ($8,000-12,000/month). Testing and reading running efficiently.

**ROI Math:**

RTM revenue: 80 patients × $130/month = $10,400/month = $124,800/year

Reading efficiency: Faster turnaround improves referring provider satisfaction, increases referrals.

Device clinic: Organized management reduces missed follow-ups, improves outcomes, reduces liability.

**Investment:**

Professional: $499/month

Implementation: $750 one-time

Device vendor integration: Custom quote

faqs.q: Is cardiac RTM different from RPM?
faqs.a: RTM (Remote Therapeutic Monitoring) covers patient-reported data: symptoms, weight logs, medication compliance. RPM (Remote Patient Monitoring) covers device-transmitted physiological data. Both can be billed, but RTM is simpler to implement (no devices needed).
Resource visibility when scheduling (stress lab, echo suite, technician). Test tracking from order through completion, reading, and communication. Pre-visit integration shows test status.

faqs.q: How does the reading queue work?
faqs.a: All pending reads in one view. Prioritized by urgency. Aging visible. Reader assignment and workload visible. Studies don't get lost in the pile.

faqs.q: Can you integrate with device vendor systems?
faqs.a: We integrate with major platforms (Medtronic CareLink, Abbott Merlin, Boston Scientific Latitude) to pull alerts into unified view. Alert triage protocols help manage volume.

faqs.q: What about same-day echo and visit?
faqs.a: Echo completion flags patient as ready. Read prioritized. Results available before cardiologist visit. Time from echo to visit significantly reduced.

faqs.q: How do you handle urgent findings?
faqs.a: Urgent studies flagged in reading queue. Critical RTM alerts (e.g., significant weight gain in CHF) trigger immediate notification. Escalation protocols configurable.

faqs.q: Does this work with EP practices?
faqs.a: Yes. Electrophysiology practices have specific needs (ablation scheduling, device management, arrhythmia monitoring) that we configure for.
Pre-procedure coordination, procedure scheduling, post-procedure monitoring all supported. Integration with cath lab systems varies by facility.
